description: xrpl payment — Send XRP, issued tokens, and MPT via Payment transactions with full support for paths, partial payments, memos, and destination tags

userStories.title: Amount parser utility (XRP, IOU, MPT)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a shared amount parsing utility so all payment-related commands parse currency amounts consistently.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/utils/amount.ts exporting parseAmount(input: string): XRPAmount | IssuedTokenAmount | MPTAmount
XRPAmount type: { type: 'xrp', drops: string } — plain decimal input multiplied by 1,000,000 and rounded to integer string; input ending in 'drops' used as-is
IssuedTokenAmount type: { type: 'iou', value: string, currency: string, issuer: string } — parsed from 'value/currency/issuer'; currency is 3 ASCII chars or 40-char hex; issuer starts with 'r'
MPTAmount type: { type: 'mpt', value: string, mpt_issuance_id: string } — parsed from 'value/<48-char-hex>'
Export toXrplAmount(parsed): string | { value, currency, issuer } | { value, mpt_issuance_id } — converts to xrpl.js-compatible amount
Export formatAmount(parsed): string — human-readable: '<n> XRP', '<value> <currency> (issued by <issuer>)', '<value> MPT:<first8chars>...'
Throws 'Error: invalid amount "<input>" — use "1.5" for XRP, "10/USD/rIssuer" for issued token, or "100/<48-char-hex>" for MPT' on invalid input
Unit tests in src/utils/amount.test.ts: XRP decimal (1.5 → drops 1500000), XRP drops suffix, IOU 3-char currency, IOU 40-char hex currency, MPT 48-char hex, invalid inputs throw
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: xrpl payment core command (XRP, autofill, submit/wait, dry-run)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to send XRP to another address so I can transfer value on the ledger.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/commands/payment.ts registered in src/commands/index.ts and added to root program in src/index.ts
xrpl payment --to <address-or-alias> --amount <amount> submits a Payment transaction
Key material (exactly one required): --seed <seed>, --mnemonic <phrase>, --account <address-or-alias> (loads from keystore; --password skips interactive prompt with insecure warning to stderr)
Calls client.autofill(tx) before signing
Default: calls client.submitAndWait(), waits for validation, prints: 'Transaction: <hash>', 'Result:      <result-code>', 'Fee:         <fee-xrp> XRP', 'Ledger:      <ledger-index>'
--no-wait flag: calls client.submit() instead, prints only 'Transaction: <hash>'
--json outputs { hash, result, fee, ledger } or just { hash } with --no-wait
--dry-run prints { tx_blob, tx: <decoded-tx-object> } as JSON to stdout without calling submit or submitAndWait
Exits 1 with error code + message to stderr on tec*/tef*/tem* result
Catches TimeoutError from submitAndWait and exits 1 with 'Error: transaction expired (LastLedgerSequence exceeded)'
Uses getNodeUrl(cmd) and resolveAccount() throughout
Alias: xrpl send
E2E test: fund two testnet accounts with wallet fund, send 1 XRP between them, assert exit 0 and stdout contains 'tesSUCCESS'
E2E test: --dry-run outputs JSON with TransactionType 'Payment' and does not submit (run account transactions after and assert no new tx)
E2E test: --no-wait exits 0 and output contains a 64-char hex hash
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Destination tag and memo support
userStories.description: As a user, I want to attach a destination tag and memo to a payment so I can identify it on exchanges or add context.
acceptanceCriteria: Add --destination-tag <n> option: sets DestinationTag field (unsigned 32-bit integer; exits 1 if out of 0–4294967295 range)
Add --memo <text> option (repeatable): each value adds a Memos entry with MemoData set to UTF-8 hex-encoded text via Buffer.from(text, 'utf8').toString('hex').toUpperCase()
Add --memo-type <hex> option: sets MemoType on the last --memo entry (optional)
Add --memo-format <hex> option: sets MemoFormat on the last --memo entry (optional)
Human-readable output shows 'Destination Tag: <n>' line when set
--json output includes destinationTag and memos fields when present
E2E test: send payment with --destination-tag 12345, run account transactions --json, assert tx has DestinationTag 12345
E2E test: send with --memo 'hello', run account transactions --json, assert Memos array is present in tx
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Issued token and MPT payments
userStories.description: As a user, I want to send issued tokens and MPTs so I can transact with non-XRP assets.
acceptanceCriteria: --amount 10/USD/rIssuer builds IOU amount object { value: '10', currency: 'USD', issuer: 'rIssuer' } via toXrplAmount(parseAmount(...))
--amount 131/0000012F...48chars builds MPT amount object { value: '131', mpt_issuance_id: '0000012F...' } via toXrplAmount(parseAmount(...))
Invalid amount format exits 1 with descriptive error from parseAmount
--send-max <amount> option: sets SendMax field; supports all three amount types via parseAmount
--deliver-min <amount> option: sets DeliverMin field and automatically adds tfPartialPayment to Flags; supports all three amount types
E2E test (IOU): establish a trust line on testnet using xrpl.js directly in test setup, send IOU payment with --amount, assert exit 0 and tesSUCCESS
E2E test (MPT): create MPT issuance in test setup, send MPT payment, assert exit 0 and tesSUCCESS
E2E test: --amount with invalid format exits 1 and stderr contains 'invalid amount'
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Path payments and payment flags
userStories.description: As a user, I want to send cross-currency path payments and control payment behaviour with flags.
acceptanceCriteria: --paths <json-or-file> option: if value ends in '.json' read from file; otherwise parse as inline JSON array; sets Paths field on tx
--partial flag: sets tfPartialPayment flag (value 0x00020000) on tx Flags
--no-ripple-direct flag: sets tfNoRippleDirect flag (value 0x00040000) on tx Flags
--limit-quality flag: sets tfLimitQuality flag (value 0x00080000) on tx Flags
When --partial is set and submitAndWait succeeds, --json output includes deliveredAmount from tx metadata (meta.delivered_amount)
--dry-run with --paths shows Paths array in decoded tx JSON output
E2E test: send with --partial --deliver-min 1/USD/rIssuer --send-max 2/USD/rIssuer on testnet, assert exit 0 (requires prior trust line setup in test)
E2E test: --paths '[]' (empty array) is accepted without error
E2E test: --partial with --json output includes deliveredAmount field
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
